summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the git commit-diff endpoint passes the unsanitized commit query parameter into a shell command via exec(), allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary operating system commands on the server.
expected_security_impact: Sending a crafted GET request to /api/git/commit-diff with a commit parameter containing shell metacharacters should cause the server to execute the injected command and include its output in the HTTP response body.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the target service, registered a user, created a project, and sent a crafted request with commit=;echo EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF. The response body contained the proof string EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF in the diff field, confirming arbitrary command execution.
verification_method: The exploit script injects a unique proof marker (EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF) via the echo command and checks whether the marker appears in the HTTP response body. This distinguishes real command execution from a generic git error or unrelated server behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: The HTTP response to the crafted commit-diff request contains the injected proof marker in the diff field.
The proof marker appears in the response only when shell metacharacter injection succeeds, confirming exec() interprets the injected command.
The server remained operational after the exploit, ruling out a startup failure or unrelated crash.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
